Description: AWS Idle Resource Agent - Bedrock Agent + read-only Lambda tools that identify unused AWS resources (last 30 days) using Cost Explorer as a starting signal, and publish a report to an existing SNS topic. Plain CloudFormation, no SAM transform - the Lambda deployment package must be uploaded to S3 first (see scripts/deploy.ps1 / deploy.sh).

NotificationTopicArn.Description: ARN of an existing SNS topic to publish the idle-resource report to (create/subscribe to this topic yourself beforehand).

BedrockModelId.Description: Bedrock foundation model ID OR inference profile ID/ARN used by the agent. Newer models (e.g. Claude Sonnet 4.5) require an inference profile - pass its ID (e.g. us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-v1:0) or full ARN here instead of the raw model ID. Must be enabled in Bedrock Model access.

CodeS3Bucket.Description: S3 bucket containing the Lambda deployment package (see scripts/deploy)

CodeS3Key.Description: S3 key of the Lambda deployment package zip

Properties.Instruction:
You are a FinOps analyst assistant. Your job is to find AWS resources that are likely unused or idle over the last 30 days and report estimated wasted spend. Steps:
1. Always call getCostByService first to see which services are actually incurring cost in the last 30 days.
2. Only call the specific idle-resource-check operations (EC2, EBS, EIP, RDS, ELB, Lambda, snapshots) for services that show non-trivial spend in step 1. Skip checks for services with near-zero cost.
3. Cross-reference each finding with the cost data to estimate a monthly savings figure per resource where possible.
4. Classify every finding as one of: "Likely idle - review", "Needs verification", or "Low confidence".
5. This tool is READ-ONLY. Never suggest deleting, stopping, or modifying resources on your own. Only report and recommend manual review.
6. When you are done analyzing, call sendReport with a well-formatted subject and a Markdown-style message body summarizing the findings (cost breakdown, list of idle candidates, estimated savings) so it can be published to the FinOps team's SNS topic. 7. If getCostByService shows non-trivial spend in a service that has no dedicated idle-check tool (e.g. S3, DynamoDB, SageMaker, Redshift, OpenSearch, NAT Gateways), call genericDescribe with an appropriate whitelisted read-only operation to investigate it instead of ignoring that spend. If genericDescribe returns a whitelist error, list that service under an "Uncovered services - recommend manual review" section in the report rather than omitting it.
Properties.Description: Finds idle/unused AWS resources using Cost Explorer + read-only checks and publishes a report to an SNS topic.

ActionGroups.Description: Read-only tools to inspect cost and resource utilization

ApiSchema.Payload: {"openapi": "3.0.0", "info": {"title": "Idle Resource Tools", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "Read-only AWS inspection tools for the idle-resource agent"}, "paths": {"/cost-by-service": {"get": {"operationId": "getCostByService", "description": "Get AWS cost grouped by service for the last 30 days from Cost Explorer", "responses": {"200": {"description": "Cost breakdown by service", "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object"}}}}}}}, "/ec2-low-utilization": {"get": {"operationId": "getEc2LowUtilization", "description": "List stopped EC2 instances or running instances with average CPU below 5% over 30 days", "responses": {"200": {"description": "List of low-utilization EC2 instances", "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object"}}}}}}}, "/unattached-ebs-volumes": {"get": {"operationId": "getUnattachedEbsVolumes", "description": "List EBS volumes in 'available' (unattached) state", "responses": {"200": {"description": "List of unattached EBS volumes", "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object"}}}}}}}, "/unassociated-eips": {"get": {"operationId": "getUnassociatedEips", "description": "List Elastic IPs not associated with any instance or network interface", "responses": {"200": {"description": "List of unassociated Elastic IPs", "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object"}}}}}}}, "/idle-rds": {"get": {"operationId": "getIdleRds", "description": "List RDS instances with near-zero database connections over the last 30 days", "responses": {"200": {"description": "List of idle RDS instances", "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object"}}}}}}}, "/idle-load-balancers": {"get": {"operationId": "getIdleLoadBalancers", "description": "List load balancers with no registered or no healthy targets", "responses": {"200": {"description": "List of idle load balancers", "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object"}}}}}}}, "/stale-lambdas": {"get": {"operationId": "getStaleLambdas", "description": "List Lambda functions with zero invocations over the last 30 days", "responses": {"200": {"description": "List of stale Lambda functions", "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object"}}}}}}}, "/old-snapshots": {"get": {"operationId": "getOldSnapshots", "description": "List EBS snapshots older than 90 days that are not referenced by any AMI", "responses": {"200": {"description": "List of old, likely-orphaned snapshots", "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object"}}}}}}}, "/send-report": {"post": {"operationId": "sendReport", "description": "Publish the final idle-resource report to the configured SNS topic", "requestBody": {"required": true, "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object", "required": ["subject", "message"], "properties": {"subject": {"type": "string", "description": "Report subject/title"}, "message": {"type": "string", "description": "Full report body (Markdown-style text is fine)"}}}}}}, "responses": {"200": {"description": "Confirmation that the report was published", "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object"}}}}}}}, "/generic-describe": {"post": {"operationId": "genericDescribe", "description": "Call a whitelisted read-only AWS API operation for a service that does not have a dedicated idle-check tool (e.g. S3, DynamoDB, SageMaker, Redshift, OpenSearch, NAT Gateways). Use this when getCostByService shows meaningful spend in a service not covered by the other tools. Only pre-approved read-only (Describe/List/Get) operations are permitted; anything else is rejected.", "requestBody": {"required": true, "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object", "required": ["service", "operation"], "properties": {"service": {"type": "string", "description": "boto3 service name, e.g. s3, dynamodb, sagemaker, redshift, es, opensearch, ec2, cloudwatch"}, "operation": {"type": "string", "description": "boto3 method name to call, e.g. list_buckets, describe_table, list_endpoints, describe_clusters"}, "params": {"type": "string", "description": "Optional JSON-encoded object of keyword arguments to pass to the boto3 method, e.g. {\"TableName\": \"my-table\"}"}}}}}}, "responses": {"200": {"description": "Result of the whitelisted read-only API call, or an error if not whitelisted", "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {"type": "object"}}}}}}}}}
